title: Mapping correlations between quantum discord and Bell non-locality
authors: Robert Okuła, Adrian Misiak, Piotr Mironowicz
abstract: We present a numerical framework for the certification and systematic analysis of the relationship between Bell nonlocality and quantum discord. By determining the minimum discord required for a bipartite state to manifest a specific Bell violation, we establish lower bounds for these correlations. We evaluate this methodology across six distinct Bell expressions, comparing their performance through the minimal discord values observed under varying intensities of white noise. Analysis of the resulting optimization landscape suggests the existence of two characteristic classes of optimized states, categorized by their minimized quantum discord.
summary: This paper presents a numerical method to map the relationship between quantum discord and Bell non-locality in two-qubit systems. It identifies the minimum amount of discord needed to maintain specific levels of Bell violation across various Bell inequalities. The results reveal how different measurement settings and noise levels influence the bounds of these quantum correlations.
main_problem: The study investigates the relationship and correlations between quantum discord and Bell non-locality, specifically seeking the minimum discord required for a bipartite state to exhibit a specific level of Bell violation.
main_result: The authors establish lower bounds for discord across six Bell expressions, finding that discord vanishes at the classical limit and that increasing measurement settings generally lowers the required discord. They also suggest the existence of two distinct classes of optimized states based on their minimized discord.
method: A numerical framework using a purification-based approach for state parametrization, combined with global optimization techniques including Basin-hopping and the COBYQA algorithm.
model_or_system: The study focuses on bipartite two-qubit mixed states, represented via a purification-based scheme with 15 optimization variables including eigenvalues and local rotation angles.
key_observables: Quantum discord (D), Bell violation (|B|), and the fraction of the Tsirelson bound (p).
important_parameters: The noise parameter (p), representing the fraction of the maximal quantum Bell value, and the number of measurement settings in different Bell inequalities.
main_assumptions: The analysis is restricted to two-level systems (qubits) and assumes the use of projective measurements for the definition of quantum discord.
figures_summary: Figures 1-3 show the lower bounds of discord for various Bell families (CHSH, ModCHSH, Braunstein-Caves, and I-family) as a function of the Bell violation fraction p; Figure 4 provides a summary comparison of all six expressions; Figures 5-7 show detailed numerical optimization landscapes and local minima for specific expressions.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of discord-nonlocality correlations, describes the numerical framework and state parametrization, presents the comparative analysis of six Bell inequalities, discusses the optimization landscape and state classification, and concludes with limitations and future directions.
why_interesting: This work provides fundamental insights into the hierarchy of non-classical correlations, which is crucial for understanding the resource requirements for quantum tasks in quantum optics and open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Lower bounds on quantum Discord for CHSH inequality, as well as (16), as a function of the fraction p of the quantum bounds |BCHSH|Q or |BMod|Q, respectively.
FIG. 2: Lower bounds on quantum Discord for the family of Braunstein-Caves inequalities, as a function of the fraction p of the quantum bounds |BBCn|Q.
FIG. 4: The summary of discord optimization results for all analyzed Bell expressions, as a function of the fraction p of the quantum bound |B|Q.
FIG. 3: Lower bounds on quantum Discord for the I1 and I2 inequalities, a function of the fraction p of the quantum bounds |BI1|Q or |BI2|Q, respectively.
FIG. 7: Combination of the results of numerical experiments undertaken to minimize the Discord function for the I1 expression, as a function of the value of the expression (Bell value).
